steps.title: Set up vertex form using the maximum point
steps.content:
The maximum height of 207.36 feet occurs at $t = 3.6$ seconds. This is the vertex of the parabola. We write the height function in vertex form:

$$h(t) = a(t - 3.6)^2 + 207.36$$

steps.title: Find the coefficient using the initial height
steps.content:
The object was launched from an initial height of 0 feet, so $h(0) = 0$. Substitute:

$$0 = a(0 - 3.6)^2 + 207.36$$

$$0 = 12.96a + 207.36$$

$$a = -16$$

So the function is $h(t) = -16(t - 3.6)^2 + 207.36$.

steps.title: Solve for the time when height equals 184.32 feet
